key_concerns: Uncertain clinical meaningfulness of 8-week RBC-TI endpoint
Low durability of transfusion independence (1-year RBC-TI only 13.6%)
No improvement in overall survival, CR/PR, or patient-reported outcomes
High rates of grade 3-4 neutropenia (71%) and thrombocytopenia (65%)
Increased infections, bleeding events, and fractures
Excessive dose modifications and unclear optimal dosing
Limited applicability to US population due to geographical imbalance
key_positives: Statistically significant improvement in 8-week and 24-week RBC-TI
Durable responses in a subset of patients (median duration 51.6 weeks in responders)
Reduction in SF3B1 variant allele frequency

evidence_notes: Durability concern: 'the point estimate of the response rate decreased as the target duration of RBC-TI increased, being only 13.6% for 1-year RBC-TI'
No OS benefit: 'The hazard ratio between the imetelstat and placebo arms was 0.98 (95% CI: 0.53, 1.82)'
Cytopenia risk: 'Grade 3-4 neutropenia occurred in 71% imetelstat vs 7% placebo'
Fracture signal: 'Fractures were documented in 61 patients (8%) who received imetelstat as monotherapy... across a variety of studies'
PRO lack of benefit: 'FDA found no compelling evidence to support improvement in fatigue or other anemia related symptoms'
US applicability uncertainty: 'the 8-week RBC-TI rate with imetelstat was much lower in the subgroup of patients enrolled at sites within the US compared to non-US sites'

questions_framing.description: FDA asks whether benefits outweigh risks, highlighting unresolved concerns with efficacy magnitude, durability, and safety.

document.title: March 14, 2024 Meeting of the Oncologic Drugs Advisory Committee- FDA Briefing Document

signal.label: STRONG_NEGATIVE

Positives: Statistically significant improvement in 8-week and 24-week RBC-TI; Durable responses in a subset of patients (median duration 51.6 weeks in responders); Reduction in SF3B1 variant allele frequency
